title: Implement binary MARC round-trip property test
description: In crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs, write the first property test: generate a record with arb_record(), serialize to binary MARC bytes, parse back with MarcReader, and assert the parsed record matches the original (same leader, same fields, same subfields). This is the single highest-value property test — it exercises the full serialization/parsing pipeline. Use proptest with default config (256 cases in CI).
close_reason: Already implemented as part of bd-aph — binary_roundtrip property test is in crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs

title: Implement targeted fixture curation in curate_fixtures.py
description: curate_fixtures.py supports --method=random (fully working) but --method=targeted raises NotImplementedError. The proposal (Phase 5.1) describes targeted selection using criteria JSON files to select records with specific characteristics: CJK content, many subfields, serials, maps, music, pre-1900, etc. This would help build a more diverse fixture collection that systematically covers MARC corner cases.

title: Gate C: End-to-End Review
description: Final review gate. Scope: The complete system. Walk through the entire tester journey: clone → setup → test fixtures → download data → test local → import → review discoveries. Key questions: Does just setup && just test work from a clean clone? Does just test-local generate discoveries? Does just import && just discoveries show results? Is the README sufficient for a new user to get started?
close_reason: All tester journey steps verified. 0 blockers. 43/45 beads closed (1 is this gate, 1 is P4 backlog). Clean clone test, architecture review, code quality review all pass. Upstream mrrc#32 filed and tracked.

title: Add just test-properties and test-properties-local recipes
description: Add two justfile recipes: test-properties runs cargo test properties (Rust) and uv run pytest suites/properties/ (Python) in CI mode. test-properties-local runs the same with MRRC_TEST_MODE=local and Hypothesis local profile (higher case counts). Also commit any proptest-regressions/ files that have been generated during development.
close_reason: Added test-properties and test-properties-local recipes, HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE env var support

title: Phase 1: Formal methods foundation (testbed)
description: Epic for Phase 1 of formal-methods-implementation-plan.md, testbed workstream only. Add proptest and hypothesis dependencies, write arb_record() strategy, implement first property tests (Rust binary round-trip + Python encoding round-trip), add justfile recipes, and configure Hypothesis profiles.
close_reason: All Phase 1 tasks complete: deps (bd-398), strategies (bd-aph), roundtrip property (bd-16q), Hypothesis profiles (bd-2yv), recipes (bd-1g4)

title: Phase 4: Test Suites
description: The actual tests. Two parallel sub-epics: Rust test suites (4a) and Python test suites (4b). All items within each sub-epic are independent. Each test module creates its associated synthetic test data as part of implementation.
close_reason: All Phase 4 test suites implemented and passing

title: 4a: Rust test suites
description: Sub-epic. All Rust integration test modules in crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/. All items are independent of each other. Each creates associated synthetic test data in data/synthetic/ as needed. All use the harness from 2a.
close_reason: All Phase 4 test suites implemented and passing

title: 4a.1: stress.rs — memory and scaling tests
description: Implement crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/stress.rs. Tests: memory_stability (no memory growth over 10M+ records), throughput_sustained (stable throughput over extended runs), thread_scaling (near-linear scaling to core count), resource_cleanup (no leaked handles/buffers). All marked #[ignore] for local-mode only. Success: memory stable ±5% over extended runs, no resource leaks, stable throughput.
close_reason: done

title: 4a.2: malformed.rs — error recovery discovery
description: Implement crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/malformed.rs. Tests: discover_malformed_patterns (catalog malformed records in IA Lendable, uses DiscoveryWriter), no_panics (no panics on any input), error_messages_useful (errors identify the problem). CI tests use synthetic malformed data in data/synthetic/malformed/. Success: no crashes on any input, catalog of malformed patterns, useful error messages.
close_reason: done

title: 4a.3: discovery.rs — edge case cataloging
description: Implement crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/discovery.rs. Tests: unusual_field_combinations (rare field patterns in LOC), extreme_values (unusually long fields, many subfields), encoding_edge_cases (unusual encoding patterns). All #[ignore] for local mode. Output: catalog of discovered edge cases for potential addition to mrrc test fixtures.
close_reason: done

title: 4a.4: encoding.rs — international character testing
description: Implement crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/encoding.rs. Tests: cjk_roundtrip (CJK records from LOC and IA), cyrillic_roundtrip (Cyrillic records from LOC), diacritics_roundtrip (European diacritics from LOC), mixed_encoding (records mixing MARC-8 and UTF-8). CI tests use synthetic encoding vectors in data/synthetic/encoding/. Success: no mojibake, encoding detection works, combining characters handled.
close_reason: done

title: 4a.5: concurrent.rs — sustained parallel load
description: Implement crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/concurrent.rs. Tests: sustained_parallel_read (16+ threads for 10M+ records), producer_consumer_stress (pipeline under sustained load), no_data_corruption (verify data integrity under load). All #[ignore] for local mode. Success: no race conditions or data corruption, no deadlocks, stable performance across thread counts.
close_reason: done

title: 4b: Python test suites
description: Sub-epic. All Python test suites in suites/. All items are independent of each other. All use the infrastructure from 2b.
close_reason: All Phase 4 test suites implemented and passing

title: 4b.1: pymarc_compat/ — real script compatibility
description: Implement suites/pymarc_compat/ test suite. Tests: test_real_scripts.py (port actual pymarc scripts from the wild and verify they work with mrrc), test_iteration_scale.py (iterator behavior over large files). All @pytest.mark.local. Success: real pymarc scripts work unmodified with mrrc, no behavioral differences at scale.

title: 4b.2: encoding/ — encoding through Python bindings

title: 4b.3: discovery/ — edge case discovery via Python
description: Implement suites/discovery/ test suite. Python-friendly interface for cataloging discovered edge cases. Writes discoveries in the standard JSON format to results/discoveries/ for import by import_run.py.
close_reason: done

title: Phase 6: Initial Data + End-to-End Validation
description: Actually run the system against real data, verify the full tester experience, and write the README. All prior components are complete; this phase is purely operational (plus the README). Two parallel tracks after 6.1: fixture curation (6.2–6.3) and discovery (6.4–6.5).
close_reason: Phase 6 complete. All 7 subtasks done: datasets downloaded (Watson + IA Lendable, LOC deferred to bd-2n9), fixtures curated via LOC SRU (586 records), discovery scan run (233 IA Lendable errors imported), full E2E walkthrough verified (2 test issues found and fixed), README.md written.

title: 6.1: Download and verify datasets
description: Download Watson (~100MB), IA Lendable (~1GB), and LOC Books All (~15GB) datasets using download_datasets.py. Run --verify to confirm integrity. These are needed for fixture curation (6.2) and discovery (6.4).
close_reason: Watson downloaded (11 .mrc, 19.8 MB). IA Lendable downloaded (106K records, 128.8 MB). LOC deferred to bd-2n9 (using SRU instead).

title: 6.2: Run initial fixture curation from LOC
description: Use curate_fixtures.py to select ~500 random + ~500 targeted records from LOC Books All. Generate manifest.json with full provenance. Target: ~1000 records, ~10MB total across bibliographic, authority, and edge_cases fixture sets.
close_reason: Curated via SRU: 386 bibliographic from LOC catalog, 150 authority from LOC NAF, 50 edge_cases from IA Lendable. Total 586 records, 680 KB.

title: 6.3: Validate and commit initial fixtures
description: Run validate_fixtures.py --strict on curated fixtures. Verify manifest sync, control number match, size budget, provenance completeness, record validity. Commit fixtures to data/fixtures/ with manifest.json files.
close_reason: All fixtures pass validate_fixtures.py --strict. 680 KB total (target <10 MB). All CI tests pass.

title: 6.4: Run first discovery pass against IA Lendable
description: Run MRRC_TEST_MODE=local cargo test malformed discovery against IA Lendable dataset. Review stdout summary for discovery counts. Verify JSON output appears in results/discoveries/.
close_reason: Discovery scan of IA Lendable: 105,852 records, 233 parse errors (0.22%). All ValueError: Truncated record. Written to results/discoveries/.

title: 6.5: Import results and verify state management
description: Run just import to process results/discoveries/ into state/. Verify: YAML files created in state/discoveries/, run YAML created in state/runs/, extracted records in state/records/, deduplication works correctly, just discoveries lists findings, just show <id> displays details.
close_reason: Import of 233 discoveries from IA Lendable to state/discoveries/ and state/runs/. Dedup verified (re-import finds 0 new). Run YAML and discovery YAMLs correct.

title: 6.6: End-to-end walkthrough of full tester workflow
description: Walk through the complete tester journey from testbed-proposal.md Typical tester session section: just test (CI mode with fixtures) → just download watson → just test-local → review discovery output → just import → just discoveries → just show <id>. Also verify BYOD path: set MRRC_CUSTOM_DATASET in .env → just test-local. Document any issues found.
close_reason: Full walkthrough complete: just test, just download watson, just test-local, just import, just discoveries, just show, just validate, just lint all work. BYOD via MRRC_WATSON env override verified. Fixed 2 issues: encoding tests now gracefully handle missing LOC datasets, memory_stability uses absolute growth check.

title: 6.7: Write README.md
description: Write README.md covering: what the testbed is and tests (scope), setup instructions (just setup), running tests in CI and local modes, downloading datasets, using BYOD, understanding discovery output, justfile recipe reference, discovery and run YAML format reference, fixture provenance and manifest format. See testbed-proposal.md Documentation section for the full outline.
close_reason: README.md written covering all sections from testbed-proposal.md Documentation spec: scope, setup, CI/local modes, downloads, BYOD, discovery workflow, justfile reference, YAML formats, fixture manifests, project structure.

title: Expand committed fixture collection toward ~1000 record target
description: The proposal targets ~1000 curated fixture records (~10MB). Current state: 586 records (386 bibliographic, 150 authority, 50 edge cases) in 788KB — about 59% of the count target and well under the size budget. Options: (1) run more random samples from IA Lendable or Watson, (2) promote existing discoveries to fixtures, (3) use curate_from_sru.py to pull records via LOC SRU. Growing the fixture set improves CI test coverage without requiring local-mode datasets.

title: Phase 5: Fixture Tooling
description: Fixture curation, validation, extraction, and promotion scripts. All scripts use the dataset abstraction from Phase 2. Independent of discovery pipeline and test suites except for promote_discovery.py, which reads discovery YAML.
close_reason: All fixture tooling scripts implemented: curate, validate, extract, promote

title: 5.1: curate_fixtures.py — initial fixture selection
description: Implement scripts/curate_fixtures.py. Two selection methods: random (randomly select N records from source) and targeted (select records matching criteria from a JSON criteria file). Generates manifest.json automatically with full provenance (source, source_url, download_date, license, per-record control_number, source_offset, selection_reason). See testbed-proposal.md Fixture Curation Strategy and Initial Fixture Curation sections for CLI interface and criteria file format.
close_reason: curate_fixtures.py implemented: random sampling with two-pass approach, MARC binary parsing, manifest.json generation. Targeted selection stubbed.

title: 5.2: validate_fixtures.py — fixture validation
description: Implement scripts/validate_fixtures.py. Validation checks: manifest sync (every record in .mrc has a manifest entry and vice versa), control number match, size budget (warning at 8MB, error at 10MB total), provenance completeness, record validity (all records parse). Supports --strict flag for CI. See testbed-proposal.md Fixture Validation section.
close_reason: validate_fixtures.py fully implemented: manifest sync, control number match, size budget, provenance completeness, record validity. --strict mode for CI.

title: 5.3: extract_record.py — record extraction utility
description: Implement scripts/extract_record.py. Extract a single record from a multi-GB file by byte offset or control number. Flags: --offset N (fast, direct seek), --control-number STR (slower, scans file), --output FILE (save extracted record), --info (display info without saving). Essential for reproducing issues found during discovery runs.
close_reason: extract_record.py implemented: --offset (fast seek), --control-number (scan), --output, --info. Full MARC binary parsing.

title: 5.4: promote_discovery.py — promote discovery to fixture
description: Implement scripts/promote_discovery.py. Takes a discovery ID, copies the extracted record from state/records/ to data/fixtures/<fixture-set>/sample.mrc, updates manifest.json with provenance (source dataset, discovery date, issue link), runs validate_fixtures.py. CLI: promote_discovery.py <disc-id> --fixture=<set> --issue <url>. Depends on discovery YAML schema from 3.1.
close_reason: promote_discovery.py implemented: promotes discovery to fixture, appends sample.mrc, updates manifest, runs validation

title: Add cross-library comparison to compare.py (mrrc vs pymarc)
description: compare.py currently compares mrrc Record objects against each other. Now that pymarc is a dev dependency (bd-3rs), extend compare_records() or add a new function to compare mrrc output against pymarc output for the same MARC record bytes. This enables catching behavioral divergences between the two libraries — the core value proposition of the pymarc_compat test suite. Should accept raw MARC bytes, parse with both libraries, and produce a structured diff of any differences.

These were specified in Phase 2b.1 of testbed-proposal.md as 'Deep record comparison utilities.' Not currently blocking anything — no test or script calls these functions — but completing them would enable cross-library output comparison (mrrc vs pymarc) and support debugging when discoveries reveal behavioral differences.

Write the first Python property test in suites/properties/test_encoding_properties.py: for arbitrary text strings, encoding through mrrc and decoding back should round-trip cleanly (using @given(st.text())). Mark local-profile tests with @pytest.mark.local.

title: Curate holdings fixture records when source data available
description: data/fixtures/holdings/ contains only .gitkeep — no sample.mrc or manifest.json. The proposal lists holdings as a fixture category but explicitly defers it in Open Questions: 'Holdings data: Deferred. No readily available public holdings datasets. Revisit if an academic library partnership materializes.' Track this so we pick it up when holdings data becomes accessible.

title: Reconcile proptest strategies between testbed and mrrc
description:
Wave A coordination point (formal-methods-implementation-plan.md lines 156-161). Both repos now have proptest strategies that generate MARC records:

- **Testbed**: arb_record() and supporting strategies in crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs
- **mrrc**: whatever strategies were written for issue #39 (binary round-trip property)

The task is to compare the two sets of strategies and decide which is canonical:

1. Read mrrc's strategies (from the PR that closed #39) and the testbed's arb_record() side by side
2. Note differences: which generates a wider variety of records? Which handles more edge cases? Which is simpler?
3. Decide: the mrrc version should become canonical since it lives in the library itself
4. Update the testbed's strategies to match mrrc's if mrrc's are better, or open an mrrc PR to upstream improvements from the testbed if ours are better
5. Once reconciled, the testbed can optionally import mrrc's strategies directly (if mrrc exposes them as test infrastructure in a future release) instead of maintaining a separate copy

This is a one-time reconciliation, not ongoing. It ensures we don't have two diverging strategy implementations generating different record shapes. Do this before starting Wave B work.
close_reason: Adopted mrrc's broader leader variation (8 fields) and more thorough roundtrip assertions. Kept testbed's control tag dedup and builder API approach. Excluded surrogates from Python text strategy.

title: Add proptest and hypothesis dependencies
description: Add proptest = "1" to crates/mrrc_testbed/Cargo.toml dev-dependencies. Add hypothesis>=6 to pyproject.toml dev dependencies. Run cargo build and uv sync to verify both resolve. Also add proptest-regressions/ to .gitignore (proptest writes regression files there that should not be committed yet — they get committed explicitly once stable).
notes: The .gitignore step in the description was incorrect — proptest-regressions/ should be committed, not ignored. They serve as permanent regression tests. This step was intentionally skipped during implementation.
close_reason: proptest 1.x added to Cargo.toml dev-deps, hypothesis 6.x added to pyproject.toml dev group

title: Populate data/synthetic/ with committed test records and generators
close_reason: All synthetic data generators and tests implemented. 6 malformed + 4 encoding .mrc files committed, 7 Rust tests passing, just generate-synthetic recipe added.

description: Everything else depends on this. Goal: just setup builds the empty crate and installs Python deps; just test runs placeholder tests. No real implementation — just structure.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: repo structure (1.1), justfile (1.2), CI workflow (1.3) all done. just setup && just test && just lint all pass.

title: 1.1: Initialize repo structure
description: Directory tree, Cargo workspace with empty crate, pyproject.toml, .gitignore, .env.example. Creates the full directory layout from testbed-proposal.md repo structure section.

title: 1.3: Set up CI workflow skeleton
description: GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) that runs on PR and push to main. Steps: validate fixtures, cargo test, uv run pytest suites/, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, ruff check. Skeleton versions that pass with empty/placeholder tests.
close_reason: CI workflow created with 4 parallel jobs: validate fixtures, Rust tests, Python tests, lint/format. Uses actions/checkout@v4, dtolnay/rust-toolchain, Swatinem/rust-cache, astral-sh/setup-uv.

title: Add pymarc as dev dependency for cross-library comparison
description: The proposal states 'pymarc is pinned to the latest release only' as a dependency, implying cross-library testing. Currently pymarc is not in pyproject.toml at all — the pymarc_compat suite tests mrrc API patterns without importing pymarc. Adding pymarc as a dev dependency would enable true side-by-side output comparison (e.g., in compare.py once implemented) and catch behavioral divergences. Low urgency since the current approach works.
close_reason: Added pymarc>=5 as dev dependency, installed 5.3.1

title: Add just fuzz-seed recipe to export fixtures as cargo-fuzz corpus
description:
## Context

Tracks Phase 4 / Wave B workstream from `formal-methods-implementation-plan.md`: the testbed contributes seed corpus to mrrc's upstream fuzzing effort. Related to upstream mrrc#90 (cargo-fuzz setup).

## What to do

Add a `just fuzz-seed` recipe that copies `data/fixtures/*.mrc` (and anything sensible from `data/fixtures/synthetic/`) into a directory suitable for seeding `cargo fuzz` corpora in the mrrc repo. Include per-target subdirectories if it makes consumption easier (e.g. `fuzz-corpus/parse_record/`, `fuzz-corpus/parse_leader/` with leader-sized slices).

Output location should be a gitignored path (e.g. `results/fuzz-corpus/`) with a short README explaining how to copy the files into mrrc's `fuzz/corpus/<target>/`.

## Done when

- `just fuzz-seed` recipe exists and runs cleanly in CI mode
- Output directory layout is documented
- A short note in `testbed-proposal.md` or `formal-methods-implementation-plan.md` explains the handoff to mrrc's fuzzer (if not already covered)
- Verified end-to-end at least once against upstream mrrc once mrrc#90 lands

## Notes

- Independent of mrrc#90 landing — recipe can be written and committed now, then exercised once fuzz targets exist upstream.
- Does not need to be fast; this is a one-shot export.

title: Gate B: Pipeline + Suites + Fixtures Review
description: Review gate. Scope: State management design, import pipeline, all test suites, fixture tooling. Key questions: Does import_run.py correctly deduplicate? Do test suites generate useful discoveries and handle all error paths without panics? Does fixture validation catch all integrity issues? Do the components work correctly in isolation? Blocks Phase 6.
close_reason: Review complete. Fixed: O(N*M) dedup→batch hash set, within-batch dedup, dead code, custom dataset name check. Filed mrrc#32 for upstream overflow bug.

title: Write arb_record() and supporting proptest strategies
description: Create crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs with arb_record() and supporting strategies (arb_leader, arb_control_field, arb_data_field, arb_subfield). Start with the simplest strategy that produces a parseable MARC record — valid leader length/base address, at least one field, proper directory entries, and record terminator. The strategy does not need to produce semantically meaningful records, just structurally valid ones that MarcReader can parse without error.
close_reason: done

title: Establish inbound workflow for fuzzer-found crash inputs as fixtures
description:
## Context

Tracks the return half of the testbed <-> mrrc fuzzing feedback loop described in `formal-methods-implementation-plan.md` (Wave B). When mrrc's fuzzer (upstream mrrc#90) finds a crashing or interesting input, the testbed should capture it as a permanent fixture with provenance, and the property/malformed tests should exercise it.

## What to do

1. Document (in `scripts/README.md` or similar) the intake steps:
   - Where to put the crashing input (`data/fixtures/fuzzer/` or similar)
   - Required `manifest.json` provenance (source: "fuzzer", upstream issue/PR link, mrrc commit sha, fuzz target name, crash summary)
   - Which tests should pick it up automatically
2. Extend `scripts/validate_manifests.py` (or equivalent) to accept the new provenance source if needed.
3. Optionally add a small helper script (e.g. `scripts/import_fuzz_crash.py`) that takes a crash file + metadata and writes the fixture + manifest entry in one shot.
4. Verify `malformed.rs` / `discovery.rs` tests exercise fixtures under the new directory (catch_unwind / parse-cleanly assertions).

## Done when

- Workflow is documented
- Manifest validation accepts fuzzer provenance
- At least one round-trip exercise (real or simulated) has been performed
- Referenced from `formal-methods-implementation-plan.md` Wave B section

## Depends on

Real exercise is gated on upstream mrrc#90 producing findings, but the plumbing can be put in place now.
design: Finalized design (2026-07-19). Corpus lives in committed sibling data/regressions/ (NOT under data/fixtures/), leaving the existing parse-clean guards (fixture_records_parse_cleanly + validate_fixtures.py, both rooted at data/fixtures/) untouched. Invariant: committed inputs must NOT panic across RecoveryModes; a clean Err is acceptable (regression guard, not parse-clean). Manifest source is a stable kind enum fuzzer|synthetic|reduction|manual; a separate optional origin names the external corpus (marc4j, pymarc, mrrc-fuzz), so no per-library category is needed. Licensing guardrail: any reduction from an externally-licensed corpus must be a license-clean minimization, never verbatim upstream bytes. Children: T1 bd-ndx.1 scaffold+schema+README (foundation); T2 bd-ndx.2 no-panic test; T3 bd-ndx.3 validator; T4 bd-ndx.4 import helper (T2-T4 depend on T1, parallelizable); T5 bd-ndx.5 bootstrap seed + round-trip + Wave B note (depends on T2/T3/T4).
close_reason: done

title: Add Hypothesis cross-layer consistency tests (Rust vs Python)
description:
## Context

Per `formal-methods-verification-strategy.md`, cross-layer consistency is **the testbed's unique verification contribution** — no other layer tests that the Rust core and the PyO3 Python bindings produce identical results for the same input. Tracks Phase 3 of `formal-methods-implementation-plan.md`.

## What to do

1. Register Hypothesis CI/local profiles in `suites/conftest.py` if not already done (CI: 200 cases, derandomize; local: 5000 cases).
2. Create `suites/properties/test_cross_layer.py` (or similar) with Hypothesis tests seeded from the fixture corpus via `@given(st.sampled_from(fixture_records))`:
   - Field count agreement between Rust and Python paths
   - Leader bytes equal
   - Control number (001) equal
   - JSON round-trip through Python matches JSON round-trip through Rust
   - Accessor results (title, author, isbns) agree between layers
3. Mark large-corpus variants with `@pytest.mark.local`.
4. When a divergence is found: if it's a mrrc bug, file upstream and add to `state/known-failures.yaml` scoped by `mrrc_source`. If it's a pymarc bug, document per the strategy doc's "pymarc is not an oracle" guidance.

## Done when

- At least 5 cross-layer property tests pass in CI mode against the fixture corpus
- Local-mode profile exercises them at 5000+ cases
- Any divergences discovered are either fixed, filed upstream, or tracked in known-failures
- `just test-properties` and `just test-properties-local` pick them up

## Notes

- Higher priority than the two fuzzing support beads because it is independent of mrrc release cadence, has no upstream dependency, and is the testbed's distinctive contribution.
- Partially overlaps with bd-2ao (mrrc vs pymarc comparison) — decide during implementation whether to merge or keep as complementary (generated-input vs hand-picked cases).

title: Add repeated control field fixture record and comparison test
description: Extract a real-world record with repeated control fields (e.g. NLS catalog BRC01945 with repeated 006/007) into data/fixtures/edge_cases/ with manifest provenance. Add a pymarc comparison test in suites/pymarc_compat/ that verifies mrrc and pymarc agree on repeated control field count, values, and round-trip preservation. References mrrc #77/#79.

title: Deduplicate testbed proptest strategies by importing from mrrc
description:
## Context

Upstream mrrc#91 landed the broadened proptest strategies and property families (leader length agreement, directory consistency, indicator/subfield shape, MARCXML/MARCJSON round-trips). The testbed's `crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs` currently maintains a local copy of `arb_record()` and friends. Once mrrc publishes a release that exposes the strategies as reusable test infrastructure, the testbed can import them instead of duplicating.

Tracks the upstream->downstream handoff described in the 'Migration path' section of `formal-methods-verification-strategy.md`.

## Depends on

- mrrc#91 merged (DONE as of 2026-04-19)
- A mrrc release (or a commit-pinned dep bump) that exposes the strategies as a public test-support module or `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))]` surface. This may or may not be in scope for the next mrrc release.

## What to do

1. Check the next mrrc release notes for strategy-export surface (or file a mrrc issue if it's not planned).
2. Bump the mrrc dep in `Cargo.toml` to the release that exposes them.
3. Replace the local `arb_record()`/`arb_field()`/etc. in `crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs` with imports from mrrc.
4. Keep any testbed-specific strategies that don't belong upstream (e.g. fixture-seeded strategies using `@given(sampled_from(fixture_records))` equivalents).
5. Verify `just test-properties` and `just test-properties-local` still pass.
6. Document the change briefly in the testbed's CHANGELOG / README section on formal methods.

## Done when

- Testbed imports strategies from mrrc instead of defining them locally (for the families shared upstream)
- No regressions in `just test-properties` / `just test-properties-local`
- Any strategies kept in the testbed are there for a documented reason (fixture-seeding, cross-layer tests, etc.)

## Notes

- Not urgent — the duplication is cheap to maintain. Priority can be re-evaluated if strategies start diverging between the two repos.
- If mrrc doesn't end up exposing the strategies as a public surface, close this bead with a note and keep the local copy canonical.

title: Gate A: Foundation Review
description: Review gate. Scope: Repo structure, configuration, dataset abstraction, test harness, DiscoveryWriter, Python infrastructure. Key questions: Is the project structure sound? Does the dataset abstraction handle all three data categories cleanly? Is the DiscoveryWriter output format right? Is the CI workflow green? Blocks Phases 3, 4, 5.
close_reason: Gate A passed. Three concerns fixed: Python _get_fixture_path now scopes by name, Python priority cascade aligned with Rust (env override → custom → downloads → fixtures), validate_fixtures.py stub created for CI. All checks pass.

title: Phase 3: Discovery Pipeline
description: The state management system and import tooling. Defines the persistent data model — getting it wrong here is expensive to fix later. Depends on DiscoveryWriter (2a.3) for the JSON format that import_run.py consumes.
close_reason: All Phase 3 tasks complete: state.py, import_run.py, justfile recipes

title: 3.1: Implement discovery and run YAML schemas
description: Define the canonical YAML schemas for discovery files (state/discoveries/*.yaml) and run files (state/runs/*.yaml). See testbed-proposal.md State Management section for exact field specs. Discovery YAML: discovery_id, discovered_at, discovered_in_run, mrrc_version, test_suite, test_name, record (sha256, control_number, source_dataset, source_offset, extracted_file), error (category, message, mrrc_error). Run YAML: run_id, started_at, completed_at, environment, datasets, results, discoveries list.
close_reason: state.py rewritten with working YAML read/write: load_discovery, load_run, list_discoveries, list_runs, save_discovery, save_run, discovery_exists (sha256 dedup).

title: 3.2: Implement state.py and import_run.py
description: Implement src/mrrc_testbed/state.py (YAML read/write for discoveries and runs) and scripts/import_run.py (reads JSON from results/discoveries/, deduplicates by sha256 against existing state/discoveries/, normalizes field names (offset_bytes → source_offset, extracted_to → extracted_file), writes YAML to state/discoveries/ and state/runs/, copies extracted records to state/records/, prints summary). See testbed-proposal.md Importing Results section.
close_reason: import_run.py implemented: JSON->YAML import, dedup, run YAML generation, --list-new mode

title: 3.3: Add justfile discovery recipes
description: Update justfile recipes for discovery workflow: import (calls import_run.py on results/discoveries/), discoveries (lists new/unreviewed discoveries), show ID (displays specific discovery YAML). Replace placeholder recipes from Phase 1.
close_reason: Justfile already has import, discoveries, and show recipes

title: Re-sync testbed to mrrc 0.9.x (testbed currently does not build)
description:
## Summary

The testbed has drifted out of sync with mrrc and **does not currently build**. mrrc
advanced from 0.7.3 to **0.9.1** while this repo was idle (0.9.0 was a breaking
release). Re-sync the testbed to mrrc 0.9.x so it builds and passes against both the
released package and a local checkout.

## Evidence (three-way version split)

- Local mrrc checkout `/Users/dchud/Documents/projects/mrrc-clean` is **v0.9.1**
  (latest release tag `v0.9.1`; MIT; edition 2024; MSRV 1.88; Python >=3.10).
- `crates/mrrc_testbed/Cargo.toml` pins `mrrc = "0.7"`.
- Root `Cargo.toml` has an (uncommitted) `[patch.crates-io] mrrc = { path = ".../mrrc-clean" }`
  = 0.9.1. Because 0.9.1 does not satisfy `"0.7"`, cargo prints
  `warning: patch mrrc v0.9.1 (...) was not used in the crate graph` and falls back
  to **crates.io mrrc 0.7.6** (`Cargo.lock` confirms `mrrc 0.7.6`).
- Against 0.7.6 `cargo test` **fails to compile** (see child: API drift).
- Python: `uv.lock` pins **mrrc 0.7.3** (`pyproject.toml` says `mrrc>=0.7`).

Net: nothing lines up — the local-checkout patch is silently ignored, Rust builds
against 0.7.6 (broken), Python runs against 0.7.3.

## Goal / acceptance

- `just test` and `just test-properties` green against released mrrc 0.9.x.
- `just use-local-mrrc ../mrrc-clean` actually points the build at the 0.9.1 checkout
  (no "patch not used" warning); `just use-released-mrrc` reverts cleanly;
  `just mrrc-status` reports correctly.
- `just lint` and `just check-known-failures` clean.

## Children

1. Fix mrrc version pins and local-patch mechanism.
2. Fix mrrc 0.9 API drift in Rust/Python suites; refresh known-failures.

This is the top priority — until it lands, the suite is only meaningful against a
hand-patched local checkout, and not even that today.
close_reason: Testbed re-synced to mrrc 0.9.1 and builds/passes against both released (crates.io/PyPI) and local checkout. See children bd-ikv.1 (pins/patch) and bd-ikv.2 (API drift + known-failures).

title: Fix mrrc version pins and local-patch mechanism
description:
## Fix mrrc version pins and the local-patch mechanism

Part of the re-sync epic. The version requirements and the `[patch.crates-io]`
mechanism are inconsistent, so the intended "test against local checkout" workflow
silently no-ops and the released build resolves an unintended version.

## Tasks

- Bump `crates/mrrc_testbed/Cargo.toml`: `mrrc = "0.7"` -> `"0.9"` (confirm exact
  minor against the 0.9.1 API).
- Make the local patch actually apply: the committed/working `[patch.crates-io]
  mrrc = { path = ... }` must satisfy the dependency requirement or cargo ignores it
  (currently: `warning: patch mrrc v0.9.1 was not used`). Decide whether the patch
  belongs in a committed `Cargo.toml` at all vs. being written on demand by
  `scripts/set_mrrc_source.py`. Verify `set_mrrc_source.py` writes a requirement that
  matches the patched version.
- Python: bump `pyproject.toml` `mrrc>=0.7` -> `>=0.9` (or pin), then `uv lock` to
  move off the locked 0.7.3.
- Verify `just mrrc-status`, `just use-local-mrrc ../mrrc-clean`,
  `just use-released-mrrc` each resolve the intended mrrc (check `Cargo.lock` /
  `uv.lock` after each).

## Evidence

`Cargo.lock` resolves `mrrc 0.7.6`; `uv.lock` resolves `mrrc 0.7.3`; mrrc-clean is
0.9.1. `cargo build --tests` emits the unused-patch warning.

## Acceptance

`cargo build --tests` against the local checkout compiles mrrc from the path (no
unused-patch warning); released mode resolves 0.9.x from crates.io/PyPI.
close_reason: Bumped Rust mrrc 0.7->0.9 and Python mrrc>=0.9; local [patch.crates-io] now satisfied (no unused-patch warning); use-local-mrrc/use-released-mrrc/mrrc-status all resolve intended 0.9.1 (crates.io + PyPI, or ../mrrc-clean path). Cargo.lock=registry 0.9.1; uv.lock=PyPI 0.9.1.

title: Fix mrrc 0.9 API drift in Rust/Python suites; refresh known-failures
description:
## Fix mrrc 0.9 API drift; refresh known-failures

Part of the re-sync epic. After the version pins are fixed to 0.9.x, the Rust suite
and supporting code will not compile/pass until reconciled with 0.9.0 breaking
changes.

## Confirmed compile errors (observed against 0.7.6; also apply to 0.9.x)

`record.control_fields.get("001")` now returns `&Vec<String>` (control fields are
repeatable), so `.as_str()` no longer exists:

- `crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/malformed.rs:121`
- `crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/malformed.rs:133`
- `crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/properties.rs:219`

Compilation stops at the first errors — **audit for further drift** once these build.

## 0.9.0 breaking changes to audit (from mrrc CHANGELOG)

- `MarcError` and the policy enums (`RecoveryMode`, `ValidationLevel`) are now
  `#[non_exhaustive]` — any exhaustive `match` needs a wildcard arm.
- `recovery::try_recover_record` **removed** (replaced by the ISO 2709 skeleton
  salvage walk). Grep the testbed for uses.
- New entry points `parse_record_from_bytes` / `parse_record_from_shared_bytes`
  (zero-copy) — consider adopting where the testbed reads single records from slices.
- Python: `record.leader` is now a property; `from_path` returns
  `MarcReader<BufReader<File>>`; `MarcError::metadata()` -> `ErrorMetadata`.
- Record shape: `control_fields` values are lists — reconcile testbed proptest
  strategies (`properties.rs`), `scripts/compare.py`, and any Python that reads
  control fields.

## mrrc 0.9 capabilities to lean on (context)

18 typed `MarcError` variants with stable codes E001-E404; two orthogonal knobs
`RecoveryMode {Strict, Lenient, Permissive}` x `ValidationLevel {Structural,
StrictMarc}`; per-record diagnostics on `record.errors` (Arc<Vec<MarcError>>);
`RecoveryCap` (default 10_000). The testbed's malformed tests can assert on specific
E-codes now instead of substring matching.

## known-failures

`state/known-failures.yaml` has one entry: `no_panics_on_any_input`, reason
"mrrc #32 arithmetic overflow ... fixed on main, not in 0.7.3", `mrrc_source:
released`. On 0.9.x #32 is fixed — re-verify and update or remove the entry; confirm
`just check-known-failures` is clean.

## Acceptance

`just test`, `just test-properties`, `just lint` green; `just check-known-failures`
reports no unexpected/stale failures; against both released 0.9.x and local checkout.
close_reason: Fixed 0.9 API drift: control_fields now Vec<String> (get_control_field accessor / repeatable roundtrip compare); EncodingAnalysis #[non_exhaustive] wildcard arm; error_messages_useful fragments updated to 0.9 wording; Python leader property + fields()/is_control_field() replacing removed control_fields(). Removed stale no_panics_on_any_input known-failure (mrrc #32 fixed in 0.9). just test/test-properties/lint/check-known-failures green vs released 0.9.1 AND ../mrrc-clean.

title: marc4j bad-record conformance corpus (LGPL-safe, fetch-on-demand)
description:
## marc4j bad-record conformance corpus (LGPL-safe, fetch-on-demand)

Exercise mrrc against marc4j's accumulated real-world bad-record test files and see
whether mrrc handles them appropriately. Idea from the repo owner; goal is to test
the library, with no requirement to ever publish the corpus.

## Motivation (why this is worth doing given existing coverage)

mrrc already has strong malformed coverage of its own: synthetic generators in the
testbed (`malformed.rs`, `data/synthetic/generators/generate_malformed.py`) plus, in
mrrc itself, 37 error fixtures pinned to `error_coverage.toml` and 15 fuzz targets.
marc4j's value is **different in kind**: a corpus of defects seen in production
library catalogs over ~20 years, each with a documented *expected permissive-reader
recovery*. It functions as a **cross-implementation oracle** — does mrrc recover what
a mature independent implementation recovers, and where it differs, is the difference
a real mrrc bug (panic, silent data loss, wrong/opaque error)?

## Licensing decision (owner OK never publishing)

- marc4j is **LGPL-2.1-or-later** (repo `LICENSE.marc4j`). Test `.java` sources carry
  no per-file header; the `.mrc/.xml/.json` data files carry no embedded notice, but
  copyright still subsists — "no header" != public domain.
- LGPL copyleft obligations attach on **distribution**, not internal use (FSF GPL
  FAQ). Chosen posture: **never commit marc4j bytes**. Fetch on demand into
  `data/downloads/marc4j/` (already gitignored) at test time; run only in **local
  mode** via `#[ignore]`. This sidesteps copyleft entirely — nothing LGPL is
  redistributed. Do NOT commit the data files or the LGPL `.java` sources. (Any
  scratch copies live in `./tmp/`, which is now gitignored.)
- The one artifact we author and CAN keep under the repo's MIT license is our own
  **expectations manifest**: metadata describing each file's defect and expected mrrc
  behavior (facts/ideas about the files, not the copyrighted bytes). Add a NOTICE
  recording provenance.

## Design (fits existing plumbing)

- Add a `marc4j` entry to `DATASET_REGISTRY` (`src/mrrc_testbed/download.py`) that
  fetches `test/resources/*.{mrc,xml,json}` from github.com/marc4j/marc4j into
  `data/downloads/marc4j/`. Either a new `github_files` method (list of raw URLs) or
  reuse `github_zip` on the repo archive, filtered to `test/resources/`.
- `datasets.rs::get_dataset` already resolves `data/downloads/<name>/` in local mode,
  so a conformance test can locate the files with existing helpers.
- Author an expectations manifest (YAML/TOML, MIT, committed): per file ->
  {defect category, marc4j test reference, expected mrrc outcome for each
  RecoveryMode x ValidationLevel: parse ok / specific E-code / count of recovered
  fields}. Where meaningful, map marc4j's severity levels (FATAL/MAJOR_ERROR/
  MINOR_ERROR/ERROR_TYPO/INFO) to mrrc E-codes.
- New local-mode test `crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/marc4j_conformance.rs` (`#[ignore]`):
  drive each file through Strict/Lenient/Permissive, assert **no panic**, compare
  actual vs manifest; unexpected results emit discoveries via the existing
  `DiscoveryWriter` pipeline for triage and possible `report_issue.py` filing upstream.
- justfile: `just download marc4j` and `just test-marc4j` (local mode).

## marc4j bad-record file reference

Raw URL prefix: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/marc4j/marc4j/master/test/resources/`

- Binary structural: `bad_leaders_10_11.mrc` (leader bytes 10-11 wrong -> force to 2);
  `bad_too_long_plus_2.mrc` (record over 5-digit length limit; later records still
  read); `bad_oversize_field_bad_directory.mrc` (field >9999 + corrupt directory);
  `bad_hathi_records.mrc` (oversize; offsets saturate at 99999);
  `datos-20161010-slice.mrc` (13-/6-byte offsets instead of 12/5);
  `unordered-directory-entries.mrc` (directory out of sequence; must not throw).
- MARC-8 / encoding: `cyrillic_capital_e.mrc`, `greekmissingcharsetchange.mrc`,
  `chinese_mangled_multibyte.mrc`, `loongboonmee.mrc` (malformed NCRs),
  `incomplete_esc_seq.mrc`, `not_unimarc_errors_3.mrc`,
  `bad-characters-in-various-fields.mrc`.
- Combined: `pride-and-prejudice-with-many-errors.mrc` (+ `pride-and-prejudice-fixed.mrc`
  as the known-good comparison target).
- MARCXML: `chabon-record-type-bad.xml`, `cruel-cruel-indicatorless-summerland.xml`,
  `chabon-bad-subfields-element.xml`, `chabon-missing-subfield-code.xml`,
  `chabon-missing-datafield-tag.xml`, `chabon-missing-controlfield-tag.xml`.
- JSON: `illegal-marc-in-json.json`.
- Manifest of constants: `test/src/org/marc4j/test/utils/StaticTestRecords.java`;
  main driver: `test/src/org/marc4j/test/PermissiveReaderTest.java`.

## Open questions (resume the brainstorm here)

- **Assertion strictness.** mrrc and marc4j use different recovery heuristics, so
  byte-for-byte equality of recovered records is NOT the bar. Recommended: first pass
  = characterization (assert no-panic, record actual mrrc behavior into the manifest);
  second pass = tighten to expected once reviewed. The signals worth filing upstream
  are crashes, silent total-loss where marc4j recovers, and missing/opaque errors.
- **Scope order.** Start with binary `.mrc` structural + MARC-8 (mrrc's core). Treat
  MARCXML/JSON defects (mrrc's marcxml/marcjson readers) as a second wave.
- **One-off spike vs durable suite.** Could spike directly against local mrrc-clean
  0.9.1 to answer "can mrrc handle these today?" fast, then decide whether to invest
  in the committed manifest + test. Decide with owner.

## Prerequisite

Needs a working 0.9.x build (see re-sync epic). Not hard-blocked — a spike can run
against the local mrrc-clean 0.9.1 checkout.

## Acceptance (when built)

`just download marc4j` populates `data/downloads/marc4j/` (untracked/gitignored);
`MRRC_TEST_MODE=local cargo test --test marc4j_conformance -- --include-ignored` runs
without panics; unexpected outcomes captured as discoveries; `git status` shows no
marc4j bytes tracked.

title: just update-known-failures does not auto-remove stale entries (doc/impl mismatch)
description:
`just update-known-failures` and its README/justfile descriptions claim it
"auto-removes stale entries" (entries that no longer fail), but the recipe runs
`check_known_failures.py --update`, whose `cmd_update` handler only REVIEWS and
reports ACTIVE/not-applicable status — it removes nothing. It is functionally
identical to `just check-known-failures`.

The actual removal path is `check_known_failures.py --update-from-results
--runner <cargo|pytest> --passing <names>` (`cmd_update_from_results`), which
needs the set of now-passing test names piped in from a test run. The pre-push
hook and CI do not prune stale entries either — they only assert that observed
failures are accounted for. So today the known-failures "cycle-out" (removing an
entry once a released mrrc ships the fix) is entirely manual.

Discovered during bd-ikv (mrrc 0.9 re-sync) when retiring the stale
`no_panics_on_any_input` / mrrc #32 entry — removal had to be done via
`--update-from-results`, not `just update-known-failures`.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Wire `update-known-failures` to actually prune: run the suites, collect
   passing test IDs, and feed them to `--update-from-results` for both runners.
2. If auto-pruning is intentionally out of scope, correct the README (L61) and
   justfile comment (L122) to describe the manual `--update-from-results` step
   and drop the "auto-remove" wording.

Locations: justfile `update-known-failures` recipe (~L122-124); README.md L61 and
the recipe table L241; scripts/check_known_failures.py `cmd_update` (L129-158)
vs `cmd_update_from_results` (L161-188).

title: Decide cross-layer test mechanism: Rust core vs PyO3 bindings (gates bd-qua)
description:
## The decision to make

bd-qua ("cross-layer consistency: Rust core vs Python bindings") assumes we can
assert that the Python binding produces the same result as "the Rust path" for a
given input. But the Python `mrrc` module IS the Rust core via PyO3 — calling
`mrrc.MARCReader(...)` in Python already runs the Rust parser. So a Python-only
Hypothesis test comparing `mrrc.X` to `mrrc.X` compares Rust to Rust and proves
nothing. Decide HOW to obtain a genuine cross-layer signal before implementing
bd-qua.

## Options

(a) Rust-emitted reference artifact. A `cargo test` / small binary walks the
fixture corpus and emits per-fixture JSON (field counts, control number 001,
24-byte leader, canonical record JSON). A Python test loads it and asserts the
Python bindings produce identical values. Only approach that genuinely tests
cross-layer (the reference is computed Rust-side, independent of the binding).
  - Pro: real coverage; catches PyO3 glue bugs (type coercion, repeatable-field
    flattening, off-by-one indexing).
  - Con: needs a Rust emitter + artifact contract (schema, location, regen step);
    the JSON comparison needs a canonical form both sides agree on.

(b) Binding self-consistency. No Rust oracle; assert the Python bindings' multiple
access paths agree for the same record (`fields()` vs `as_dict()` vs `to_json()`;
accessors vs low-level field reads).
  - Pro: pure-Python, simple, no cross-process artifact.
  - Con: NOT truly cross-layer — if Rust core and binding share the same wrong
    behavior it won't catch it. Weaker guarantee.

Leaning: (a) is the honest realization of bd-qua's purpose; (b) is a cheaper
partial substitute. Could combine: (a) for core invariants (count, 001, leader,
JSON), (b) for accessor breadth.

## 0.9 wrinkles that make the choice matter

- Python `fields()` returns control + data together; Rust separates `fields` and
  `control_fields`. A "field count" comparison must define which, and a Rust
  artifact must expose both.
- Control fields are repeatable (Vec<String>); the reference format must carry
  all values per tag, not just the first.
- `mrrc.Record.from_bytes` does not exist in 0.9 (bytes -> Record via
  `MARCReader` / `read`); the mechanism must use real 0.9 entry points.

## Also decide

- Reuse `src/mrrc_testbed/compare.py` (now 0.9-correct structured Record diff) as
  the comparison engine vs. hand-rolled asserts.
- Relationship to bd-2ao (mrrc vs pymarc): shared harness or complementary.

Context captured 2026-07-19 while reviewing bd-qua after the mrrc 0.9 re-sync
(bd-ikv). Pick the mechanism here, then refresh + implement bd-qua.

title: Scaffold data/regressions corpus: dir, manifest schema, README
description: Create committed sibling dir data/regressions/ (with .gitkeep; only downloads/ and local/ are gitignored, so it commits by default). Define manifest.json schema: top-level {description, schema_version, records[]}; per-entry {filename, sha256, source, origin, fuzz_target, mrrc_source, upstream_issue, discovered, crash_summary, expected_behavior, notes}. source is a stable kind enum fuzzer|synthetic|reduction|manual; origin optionally names the external corpus (marc4j, pymarc, mrrc-fuzz). Write data/regressions/README.md stating the invariant (inputs must NOT panic; a clean Err is acceptable; this is NOT parse-clean) and the licensing guardrail: any reduction from an externally-licensed corpus must be a license-clean minimization, never verbatim upstream bytes. Foundation for T2-T4.
close_reason: done

title: Add CI-safe no-panic regression test (regressions.rs)
description: New crates/mrrc_testbed/tests/regressions.rs with regression_inputs_no_panic() and no ignore attribute, so it runs in just test and CI. For each file in data/regressions/, for each RecoveryMode (Strict/Lenient/Permissive), read bytes and run MarcReader under panic catch_unwind; assert no panic. Err returns are acceptable and expected. Empty dir prints and returns (like fixture_records_parse_cleanly). Model on synthetic_malformed_records in malformed.rs but reading committed files. Depends on T1.
close_reason: done

title: Add regression manifest validator + wire into just validate
description: New scripts/validate_regressions.py: verify file-to-manifest sync both directions, sha256 matches bytes, and required provenance present (source, mrrc_source, crash_summary, expected_behavior). No structural or parse-clean assertion by design. Wire into the just validate recipe alongside validate_fixtures.py. Kept separate from validate_fixtures.py because schema and semantics diverge. Depends on T1.
close_reason: done

title: Add import helper import_regression.py + just add-regression
description: Add scripts/import_regression.py plus a just add-regression recipe. It copies the input file to the next crash_NNNN.mrc, computes sha256, appends a manifest entry with the current date, prints the entry for review, and reminds to run just validate and cargo test. Flags: --source (default fuzzer), --target, --mrrc-sha, --issue, --summary, --origin. Follows the promote_discovery.py precedent. Depends on T1.
close_reason: done

Add a short Regression corpus intake note to formal-methods-implementation-plan.md Wave B. Satisfies the bd-ndx Done-when round-trip exercise and plan reference. Depends on T2, T3, T4.

title: CI Rust-tests job masks cargo test exit code (reports green on failure)
description: In .github/workflows/ci.yml the Run-cargo-test step (id: cargo-test, ~line 40) sets continue-on-error: true and ends with: cargo test 2>&1 | tee cargo-test-output.txt ; echo exit_code=PIPESTATUS >> GITHUB_OUTPUT (~line 45). Because the trailing echo succeeds, the STEP exit status is 0, so steps.cargo-test.outcome is always success. Effects: (1) the Rust-tests job goes green even when cargo test fails to compile or a test fails; (2) the downstream known-failures step guarded by if steps.cargo-test.outcome == failure (~line 47) is skipped and the captured exit_code output is never inspected. Demonstrated by PR #6 (sha2 0.11 bump): the same E0277 compile error failed the Lint clippy step but the Rust-tests job reported success. Net: cargo clippy in the Lint job is currently the only real Rust gate. The pytest job (~line 73) uses the same continue-on-error pattern but DOES re-raise via exit exit_code (~line 81), so only the Rust job is broken. Fix: add a step after the known-failures check that reads steps.cargo-test.outputs.exit_code and exits non-zero when not 0 (mirror the pytest job), or restructure so a real failure fails the job. Priority P2: a hole in the CI safety net (a test-only regression would pass CI unnoticed), mitigated for now by the Lint clippy gate and the local pre-push hook. Found during PR #6 CI triage.
